title: Trigger reactivity through assignment or runes
description: Svelte's compiler reacts to assignments, not to in-place mutation, so updates must reassign or use $state.
body: Use when state changes in a component but the DOM does not update. Do not push/splice an array or set a nested object field and expect a re-render in legacy mode. Actions: either reassign (arr = [...arr, x]) so the compiler sees the dependency, or adopt the $state rune whose proxy tracks deep mutation; pick one model per component and stay consistent. Validation signals include a @testing-library/svelte test asserting the DOM reflects the change after the update. Risk boundary: silently relying on mutation is a correctness bug that hides until production data shapes change; when migrating between legacy and runes, do it per-component, not half-way.
